microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user likes food served warm without overcooking. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND physically fatigued AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 3 min BECAUSE hotter food feels more satisfying when effort is high. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 1 min BECAUSE speed matters more than ideal temperature. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the left or right AND neutral AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user wants normal warmth without delaying the interaction. IF eating while working on a laptop or tablet in front AND in a hurry AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 1 min BECAUSE efficiency is prioritized during task-focused meals. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND fruit or a cold dessert THEN no microwave BECAUSE those foods are preferred cold. IF any dining context AND relaxed with ample time AND dense leftovers or meat-heavy foods THEN 3 min BECAUSE extra heating improves comfort and palatability.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND foods without sauces THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE there is no competing visual target. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the left or right AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN minimize left occlusion or minimize right occlusion BECAUSE social visibility still wins over convenience under this user's tie-break policy. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE comfort and simplicity matter more than view preservation when no one else is present.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND bite-sized meats or firm foods THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user favors reliable bite acquisition.
